evidences.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Draw: The official pots, seeding and restrictions explained | FOX 4 Dallas-Fort Worth
evidences.content: Pot 1 includes the strongest teams and the host nations: Canada, Mexico, the United States, Spain, Argentina, France, England, Brazil, Portugal, the Netherlands, Belgium and Germany. Pot 2 includes additional top-ranked countries: Croatia, Morocco, Colombia, Japan and Switzerland. Pot 3 represents mid-tier nations, while Pot 4 includes lower-ranked teams and nations that have not yet qualified through playoff tournaments. From there, FIFA creates 12 World Cup groups, labeled A through L, with four teams in each. ## Group selections Dig deeper: The host nations receive special placement and are assigned to predetermined groups rather than being drawn at random. Once the hosts are slotted, the draw begins. [...] One team from Pot 1 is placed into each of the 12 groups, followed by one team from Pot 2, Pot 3 and Pot 4. By the end of the process, each group contains exactly one team from each pot. However, regional restrictions add another layer. > View this post on Instagram > > A post shared by FIFA (@fifa) ## Regional rules What we know: FIFA rules prevent teams from the same region from landing in the same group. The World Cup knockout stage begins after the group stage and every match must produce a winner. Teams that lose are eliminated, while winning teams continue progressing toward the final. The 2026 tournament introduces a Round of 32 before the Round of 16. According to official organiser’s tournament schedule, the Round of 32 takes place between June 28 and July 3 before the later knockout rounds continue. evidences.content: The 2026 World Cup will be the first to take place with 48 teams, expanded from 32 in 2022. For the group stage, teams will be divided into twelve groups of four teams. The top two teams from each group plus the eight best third place teams will proceed to the (new) Round of 32. All matches after the group stage will be knockout format. A total of 104 matches will be played, up from 64 at the 2022 edition. The 2026 World Cup groups were finalized following the Final Draw on December 5, 2025 and the final qualifying matches on March 31, 2026. The games will take place in16 venuesthroughout Canada, Mexico, and the USA. The 2026 World Cup Final will take place on July 19, 2026. evidences.title: How FIFA World Cup rules on extra time and tiebreakers work for 2026
evidences.content: ## Added time, extra time and tiebreaker rules Each match is 90 minutes, split into two 45-minute halves. At the end of each half, however, there will be added time to make up for stoppages in play — such as when a player is injured, or there is a lengthy replay review — when the clock keeps running. If a match is still tied at the end of regulation, it goes into extra time with 30 minutes added. This time will be divided into two 15-minute periods, with a short break in between, and added time also applied to those two mini-halves. Extra time only applies to the games in the round of 32, round of 16, the quarterfinals, semifinals, third-place match and the final. Group Stage matches can end in a draw.

evidences.title: How Does the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw Work? Rules, Format ...
evidences.content: Pot 1 teams will be the first to be drawn in random groups, except for the host nations, who know which group they will be in: Mexico (Group A), Canada (Group B), and the United States (Group D). Then, teams from Pot 2, 3, and 4 will be randomly drawn into the groups to complete the 12 groups for the 2026 World Cup. Conductors of the 2026 World Cup Draw will notify fans if there is a constraint if a team is randomly drawn in a group that also includes a team from the same confederation. In case of a constraint, the team will be moved to the next group available. The draw concludes once all 48 teams are assigned a group, with the official schedule of the matches being decided by FIFA on a later date. [...] The format of the World Cup draw is similar to previous editions. The draw will be longer (two hours) due to the increase in teams for the 2026 World Cup. Here is how to watch. FIFA President Gianni Infantino on a visit to the Kennedy Center ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 draw in Washington, DC. ## 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw Explained There are four pots, each containing 12 teams based on the FIFA rankings, except the 2026 World Cup hosts, Mexico, the United States, and Canada....

evidences.title: Procedures and pots | Final Draw | FIFA World Cup 2026
evidences.content: For pots 2, 3 and 4, each team’s group position will be determined according to a predefined allocation pattern. This pattern is detailed in the table included in the draw procedures document. A team’s position within the group will then be determined by the pot from which they are drawn and by the group into which they are drawn. Image 6 In principle, no group will have more than one team from the same confederation drawn into it. This applies to all confederations except UEFA, which is represented by 16 teams. Each group must have at least one, but no more than two UEFA teams drawn into it. [...] To ensure competitive balance, two separate pathways to the semi-finals have been established when developing the match schedule. In order to have a balanced distribution of the teams, the four highest-ranked teams in the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s Ranking, when drawn, will have the following constraints: the highest-ranked team (Spain) and the second highest-ranked team (Argentina) will be randomly drawn into opposite pathways, and the same principle will apply to the third (France) and fourth (England) highest-ranked teams. This will ensure that, should they win their groups, the two high...

evidences.title: How does the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw work?
evidences.content: So let's break down what actually happens in the World Cup final draw. There are 4 pots that the 48 teams are separated into. Pot one includes the 3 host nations, the US, Canada, and Mexico, and then the top 9 teams in the FIFA world rankings, teams like Spain, Argentina, and Brazil. Pot 2 has the next 12 teams in the rankings, pot 3, the next 12 after that, and pot 4 has the bottom 12 teams in the field. During the draw, they will sort these pots into the. 12 total groups and each group will have one team from each pot. A team from pot one will likely be the favorite to win that group. Pot two, the likeliest challenger, and a team from pot 3 and pot 4 as well. Those 12 groups will be labeled Group A through Group L, and each group will play a round robin to determine which teams advance

evidences.title: Curiosities from the World Cup group stage - FIFA
evidences.content: When it comes to World Cup group stages, there is Brazil and then there is everyone else. The _Seleção_ hold the record for the most group wins, having finished top of their section 15 times. Germany are second with 12 group titles, ahead of Argentina, the Netherlands and Spain with six each. Brazil also hold the record for the most consecutive group-stage triumphs, having topped their group in each of the last 11 editions, a streak that began in 1982. [...] Despite their disappointment in 1982, Hungary still hold the record for the most goals scored in a first-round group stage. At Switzerland 1954, in a format where teams played only two matches, the Magical Magyars scored 17 goals thanks to emphatic victories over Korea Republic (9-0) and eventual champions West Germany (8-3). The next-best attacking performances in group-stage history belong to Hungary in 1982 and Poland in 1974 with 12 goals, followed by France in 1958, Brazil in 2002 and Germany in 2002 with 11. evidences.title: Spain World Cup history, records and 2026 fixtures
evidences.content: Prior to that, the country most notably secured a highly regarded fourth-place finish at Brazil 1950. This included topping Group 2 with a clean sweep of victories: 3-1 against the USA, 2-0 against Chile and 1-0 against England.
